observedSectionWindows.note: Scope, background, calculation context, and segment-length framing.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on the active AG 46 PDF only.
Do not widen into later guideline files.
Keep the page-image wording backstop because the text layer is encoding-noisy and stable line references are not expected.
Use page locators rather than line locators when the text layer does not support stable line mapping.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
Keep AG 47 out of scope for this wave.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No later guideline files in the same wave.
AG 47 remains out of scope for this wave.

topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the opening guidance and calculation context together as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.title: AG 46 segment-length interpretation slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the entire 2-page guideline as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the entire 2-page guideline as one narrow review-only slice and keep AG 47 outside the batch.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the scope, formula, and applicability language as regulatory text when it directs what the company must do.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat table labels, nonforfeiture terms, and cash-benefit references as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat the opening formula guidance and the applicability framework as structural guidance for the guideline's calculation method.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the adjusted-premium and nonforfeiture-net-level-premium text as calculation structure.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat the formula passages and premium references as formula context unless explicit computational rules are given.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat any requirement that fixes a premium basis or benefit basis as a prescribed assumption when the source explicitly does so.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Treat company-estimate language as company / prudent-estimate assumptions only when the source explicitly makes such a choice.
reviewStandards.scenarioOrStochasticRequirement: Treat scenario language, if any appears, as stochastic requirement language only when the source explicitly uses it.
reviewStandards.assetModelingJudgment: Treat any asset-modeling choice as asset-modeling judgment only when the source explicitly makes such a choice.
reviewStandards.hedgingOrRiskMitigation: Treat hedge language as hedging / risk mitigation only if it appears in the source.
reviewStandards.reportingRequirement: Treat any certification, report, or statutory-reporting instruction as a reporting requirement if it appears in the source.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat support, memo, or review instructions as documentation expectations if they appear in the source.
reviewStandards.governanceOrControlExpectation: Treat commissioner approval, review, and control language as governance or control expectations.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Nonforfeiture Law and related guidance as cross-reference context unless operationalized.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title, introduction, and section labels as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the encoded text layer, the formula-heavy wording, and the applicability boundary as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 46 plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
Every planned batch must remain review-only by default.
The source posture must stay explicitly active.
Each planned batch must have a matching batch definition.
The AG 46 batch definition file must stay synchronized with the plan.
The page-image backstop and page-locator-only posture must stay visible for the noisy text layer.
AG 47 must remain out of scope for this wave.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag46-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-152 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 46 definition file so the shared runner can find batch-152.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 46 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.
The page-image wording backstop should remain visible in the review packet because the AG 46 text layer is noisy and line references are not expected.

notes: Controlled AG 46 planning artifact. Planned batch 152 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
